title: Charred Crispy Slovakian Potatoes Stone-Bowl-Dolsot
description: A traditional Slovakian dish featuring crispy, charred potatoes in a stone bowl, perfect for a high-protein breakfast or lunch. This recipe is easy to make and requires minimal ingredients.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: use a neutral-tasting oil for best results

instructions: Wash and peel 4 large potatoes, then cut into 1-inch wedges.
In a large bowl, toss potatoes with 1 tablespoon olive oil, salt, and black pepper until evenly coated.
Spread potatoes in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes.
While potatoes are roasting, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add chopped chives and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until wilted.
In a large stone bowl, combine cooked potatoes, garlic-chive mixture, crumbled feta cheese, and a dollop of Greek yogurt.
Crack 4 eggs into the bowl and use a fork to scramble them with the potato mixture.
Transfer the bowl to the preheated oven and bake for an additional 5-7 minutes, until the eggs are cooked through.
Remove from oven and serve hot, garnished with additional chives if desired.
